summary: DeVault reacts with fury to a Vice report revealing that a US military unit conducting drone strikes purchased location data harvested from ordinary smartphone apps, including Muslim-oriented ones. He argues this represents the ultimate consequence of the corporate surveillance and adtech industry: user data collected for advertising purposes being weaponized to literally kill people. He calls on individual programmers to take personal moral responsibility for the surveillance infrastructure they build, rejecting the excuse that employees are just trying to feed their families. The post frames participation in corporate surveillance as complicity in murder.
key_points: Location data from ordinary apps like Quran readers was sold through data brokers to the Iowa National Guard for use in drone strike targeting
The adtech and corporate surveillance pipeline has no floor on how depraved its uses can become — data collected for ads ends up enabling killings
Individual programmers bear moral responsibility for the surveillance systems they build, regardless of their employer's stated intentions
The common excuse of 'feeding my family' rings hollow when someone else's family is destroyed by the technology you built
Data hoards are treated as corporate assets and can be sold to anyone, meaning developers cannot know or control future uses
money_quotes: Corporate surveillance and adtech has turned your phone into an informant against you and brought about the actual murder of the user.
The app you installed is selling your GPS data so it can be used to kill you.
It seems I have to revise my sermon to "don't murder the user"!
If you work at a company which surveilles its users, you are complicit in these murders.
Do you really know what that database is being used for, or who it's being sold to, or who it might be sold to in the future?
Well, thanks to your work, a child you've never met was orphaned, and doesn't have a family anymore. Who's going to feed them?
key_insight: The corporate surveillance industry's data pipelines have no ethical floor — data collected to serve ads can and does end up being used to kill people, making every participant in that pipeline morally complicit.
